strategy: Protect your Feint with Indemnity. Any Feint on Loother triggers a Cleanse Ward that removes all unprotected Traps and Prisms, so Indemnity keeps your key trap alive.
Or sidestep the cheat entirely: Mass Feint (+75%) and the Balance version of Feint do not trigger the trap wipe, so those are safe to stack.
Do not heal mid-setup. Healing (including pet heals) makes him cast Mass Infection (-65%), gutting your next attack, so heal before you commit or bring Cleanse Charm.
He is Death with 30% Death resist and takes 15% boosted Life, so use a Life hitter or Life prisms; watch his random Steal Health and his multi-target Poison (45+450).
Come in on time. A late wizard eats Shadow Bad Juju, and at Round 1 he summons two Fangs (Guards and/or Assaulters), so plan to clear minions with AoE.
Set up behind Indemnity or Mass Feint plus blades, avoid healing into the Infection, then AoE Loother and his Fangs down together.
cheatNotes: A late-joining wizard is hit with Shadow Bad Juju, so be present when the fight starts.
On Round 1 he summons two minions in a random mix of Fangs Guards and Fangs Assaulters; expect an AoE to clear them.
Placing a Feint on him triggers a Cleanse Ward that strips all unprotected Traps and Prisms. Protect your Feint with Indemnity.
Mass Feint (+75%) does not trigger the trap-removal cheat, so it is a safe trap source.
The Balance version of Feint also does not trigger the cheat.
Indemnity on your Feint stops it from being removed by the Cleanse Ward.
Any heal you cast makes him drop Mass Infection (-65%) on your team, so avoid healing right before your hit.
Pet heal procs also set off the Mass Infection cheat, so mind your pet.
He randomly cheat-casts Steal Health to top himself off, so expect some self-healing.
